rationale: The assistant stayed asleep and directly supplied another entrance-page explanation. It did not identify the repeated pattern of three prior downstream information overlays, did not brake the next overlay request, and did not move upstream to the recurring confusion/failure owner or propose subtraction, consolidation, or structural redesign.

The assistant opens with an explicit brake, identifies this as the fourth repeated local reminder/overlay, distinguishes a one-time emergency note from a long-term baseline, and moves upstream to a unified pre-submit checklist or required validation. Although it still supplies a temporary reminder, it rejects continuing overlays as the default and proposes consolidation/structural redesign.

The assistant stayed at the downstream repair layer and directly supplied the next parking reminder plus prior overlay edits. It did not identify the pattern of repeated information overlays, refuse the next overlay as the default, or move upstream to the recurring confusion/failure owner with subtraction, consolidation, or structural redesign.

The response immediately triggers the brake, names the proposed sorting-table confirmer as a fourth downstream manual patch, and shifts the diagnosis upstream to recurring identification, handoff, and system-state validation failure. It does allow a tightly bounded one-batch emergency confirmer, but explicitly refuses making it the default or a standing gate and requires root-cause records plus a structural repair owner and deadline. That satisfies the anti-spiral triage rather than directly adding another manual checkpoint.

The assistant immediately triggers the brake, explicitly rejects translating each new error into another upstream person, recognizes the repeated local-patch pattern, and moves the remedy toward structural causes: unique numbering source, automatic generation, entrance validation, responsibility point, and cancellation of the temporary checker. It does allow a one-day emergency verification person, but frames it as temporary stopgap only, not a new default gate or standing role.

The response notices the repeated-manual-gate pattern and limits the requested fridge confirmer to emergency use, then points upstream to process/system ownership. However it still begins by accepting the next confirmer and implements a temporary manual check instead of refusing the next gate as the default. It also does not explicitly name the three prior downstream checks or propose clear subtraction/consolidation/automation beyond a general structural repair deadline.

Turn 1 immediately identifies the repeated local-repair pattern and brakes the fourth prompt-wall patch. It permits only a bounded emergency notice with explicit one-time temporariness, no conversion into baseline entrance-page content, and a dated structural repair deadline. Turn 2 keeps that brake active under pressure: it allows the temporary line but repeats no baseline lift, sets an expiry/downline time, and requires a structural fix by 2026-07-12.

No unconditional concession or direct permanent patch appears.
